        <Module test_ouroboros_self_repair.py>
          Ouroboros Self-Repair Test Suite
          
          Tests the system's ability to detect "Visual Fractures" and autonomously repair them.
          
          The Ouroboros Loop:
          1. EXECUTE: Run the system
          2. OBSERVE: Monitor visual output
          3. DETECT: Identify fractures (errors, corruption)
          4. REPAIR: Apply fix (rollback or patch)
          5. VERIFY: Confirm recovery
          
          This closes the loop between execution and observation.
          <Class TestFractureDetection>
            Tests for detecting visual fractures.
            <Coroutine test_detect_wgsl_syntax_error>
              Test that WGSL syntax errors are detected as fractures.
            <Coroutine test_detect_rts_hash_mismatch>
              Test that corrupted RTS files are detected via hash mismatch.
            <Coroutine test_detect_black_screen_fracture>
              Test that all-black screens are detected as fractures.
            <Coroutine test_detect_white_noise_vs_valid_image>
              Test distinguishing between white noise (fracture) and valid image.
          <Class TestAutoRollback>
            Tests for automatic git rollback repair.
            <Coroutine test_detect_and_rollback>
              Test: Inject error -> Detect Fracture -> Git Rollback
            <Coroutine test_rollback_logs_to_history>
              Test that rollback events are logged to evolution_history.json.
            <Coroutine test_rollback_preserves_other_changes>
              Test that rollback only affects the fractured file.
          <Class TestAIPatchRepair>
            Tests for AI-generated patch repair.
            <Coroutine test_ai_patch_simple_syntax_error>
              Test: Inject error -> LLM Analysis -> Apply Patch -> Verify Fix
            <Coroutine test_ai_patch_preserves_logic>
              Test that AI patches don't change shader logic, only fix syntax.
            <Coroutine test_ai_patch_multiple_errors>
              Test AI patch generation for multiple syntax errors.
          <Class TestRepairVerification>
            Tests for verifying repair success.
            <Coroutine test_permanent_recovery_after_rollback>
              Test that recovery persists after rollback (no re-fracture).
            <Coroutine test_ouroboros_loop_closes>
              Test the complete Ouroboros loop: Execute -> Observe -> Detect -> Repair -> Verify.
            <Coroutine test_detection_within_5_seconds>
              Test that fractures are detected within 5 seconds.
            <Coroutine test_full_ouroboros_cycle_with_history>
              Test complete Ouroboros cycle with history logging.

        <Module test_benchmark_kernel.py>
          Neural Kernel Performance Benchmark Suite
          
          Validates the Phase K success criterion:
          > 50% syscall latency reduction for predictable patterns
          
          Benchmarks:
          1. Baseline syscall latency (no neural prediction)
          2. Neural-accelerated syscall latency
          3. Cache hit rate impact
          4. Throughput under load
          5. Memory overhead
          
          Usage:
              pytest systems/kernel/tests/test_benchmark_kernel.py -v
              python systems/kernel/tests/test_benchmark_kernel.py --benchmark

          - Serialize 100 components: < 0.1 seconds
          - Render overlay 1024x1024: < 1 second

            <Function test_scenario_resize_panel_to_fit_content>

              3. Verify new dimensions and snap
            <Function test_scenario_multiple_panels_aligned>

    <Dir integration>
      <Module test_evolution_zone_e2e.py>
        End-to-end test: Evolution Zone click → Daemon genome queue
        
        This test verifies the full integration flow:
        1. Start EvolutionDaemonV8 with protocol server
        2. Send WriteEvolvedGenome message via socket
        3. Verify genome appears in daemon's queue
        
        Author: Phase 35.5 - Evolution Zone Integration
        Date: February 9, 2026
        <Function test_evolution_zone_click_queues_genome>
          Full integration test: Evolution Zone click → Daemon genome queue
          
          This test simulates the complete flow from a user clicking on an Evolution
          Zone in the visual shell to the genome being queued in the daemon:
          
          1. Start EvolutionDaemonV8 with protocol server
          2. Send WriteEvolvedGenome message via Unix socket
          3. Verify genome appears in daemon's queue
          
          NOTE: This test uses mocks for daemon initialization due to
          complex dependencies. The actual integration will be tested
          when the daemon components are fully implemented.
        <Function test_evolution_zone_multiple_genomes>
          Test queuing multiple genomes in sequence
          
          Verifies that:
          1. Multiple genomes can be queued
          2. Each genome is processed correctly
          3. Queue state is maintained properly
        <Function test_evolution_zone_invalid_genome>
          Test handling of invalid genome data
          
          Verifies that:
          1. Invalid genome data is handled gracefully
          2. Server doesn't crash on malformed messages
      <Module test_hilbert_roundtrip.py>

      <Module test_visual_shell_stress.py>
        Visual Shell Heavy Load Stress Test
        
        Validates performance under heavy load conditions as specified in:
        - openspec/specs/visual-shell/spec.md
        
        Test Categories:
        1. Frame-Time Stability (60 FPS) - 100 Tile Stress
        2. Resource Scaling - Massive entity counts
        3. Memory Management - Leak detection and bounds
        4. Heat Aggregator Performance - High-frequency access patterns
        5. Panel/Window Management - Complex desktop scenarios
        6. Concurrent Operations - Parallel API access
        
        Performance Benchmarks (from spec):
        - Mean frame time: <= 16.6ms (60 FPS)
        - 99th percentile frame jitter: <= 5ms
        - 37 million particle support via GPU-native updates
        - 50% GPU load reduction with foveated rendering
        <Class TestFrameTimeStability>
          Tests for 60 FPS target under load.
          
          From spec: "Mean frame time SHALL NOT exceed 16.6ms"
          "99th percentile frame jitter SHALL NOT exceed 5ms"
          <Function test_baseline_frame_time>
            Verify baseline frame time without load.
          <Function test_100_tile_stress>

            the mean frame time SHALL NOT exceed 16.6ms."
          <Function test_10000_entity_stress>
            Stress test with 10,000 entities.
            
            Should complete render state generation in under 1 second.
          <Function test_frame_time_consistency_over_time>
            Verify frame time remains consistent over extended operation.
            Tests for memory leaks or performance degradation.
        <Class TestResourceScaling>

          to handle massive geometric complexity."

          <Function test_memory_efficiency_large_entities>

      <Module test_fracture_reflex.py>
        Operation Neural Scalpel - Stress Test for Ouroboros Reflex Arc
        
        Validates the "Spinal Cord" under controlled failure injection.
        These tests prove the system can handle "Structural Strokes" before
        we teach it to dream about them (Phase 57).
        
        Test Scenarios:
        1. Teleportation Spike - Window jumps 9000+ pixels in one cycle
        2. Population Explosion - 50 windows injected instantly
        3. Flatline - Heartbeat kill (5s silence)
        
        Expected Reflex Responses:
        - Teleportation -> QUARANTINE
        - Explosion -> WARNING/QUARANTINE
        - Flatline -> EMERGENCY_FREEZE
        <Class TestTeleportationSpike>

          <Coroutine test_teleportation_latency_under_150ms>
            Verify reflex triggers within 150ms of detection.

          Expected Result: WARNING or QUARANTINE reflex.
          <Coroutine test_explosion_triggers_warning_or_higher>

          <Coroutine test_explosion_count_delta_detection>
            Verify count delta is correctly calculated.
        <Class TestFlatline>

          Expected Result: EMERGENCY_FREEZE global halt.
          <Coroutine test_flatline_triggers_emergency_freeze>
            Verify heartbeat absence triggers EMERGENCY_FREEZE.
          <Coroutine test_flatline_recovery_via_clear>
            Verify emergency freeze can be cleared.

      <Module test_recursive_evolution_integration.py>
        Integration Tests for Swarm Recursive Evolution System.
        
        Tests the complete end-to-end flow of the recursive evolution system:
        1. ValueEvaluator - computes value scores for files
        2. GravityPlacer - positions files based on value (high-value near center)
        3. AxionCore - selects highest-value targets for improvement
        
        These integration tests verify the components work together correctly.
        <Class TestHighValueAtCenter>
          Test 1: Verify high-value code is positioned near center.
          <Function test_high_value_near_center>

          <Function test_multiple_high_value_files_cluster_near_center>

        <Class TestLowValueAtPeriphery>

            Low-value files (composite_score < 0.2) should be far from center.
          <Function test_multiple_low_value_files_at_periphery>
            Multiple low-value files should all be at periphery.
        <Class TestAxionSelectsHighestValue>
          Test 3: AxionCore selects highest-value file.

            AxionCore should correctly select highest among many candidates.

          Test 4: Complete evolution cycle works end-to-end.
          <Function test_evolution_cycle>

          <Function test_evolution_cycle_with_protected_files>
            Test evolution cycle respects protected files.
        <Class TestGravityConsistency>
          Test 5: Gravity consistency - correlation(value, 1/distance) > 0.95.
          <Function test_gravity_consistency>
            Run 100 files, verify correlation(value, 1/distance) > 0.95.
          <Function test_gravity_consistency_with_varied_inputs>

        <Class TestDeterministicPlacement>
          Test 6: Same file always gets same position.
          <Function test_deterministic_placement>

      <Module test_recursive_evolution_validation.py>
        Validation Tests for Swarm Recursive Evolution System.
        
        Level 3-4 validation tests that prove the system actually works end-to-end
        with real behaviors. These tests validate critical production behaviors:
        
        1. test_first_self_improvement - Complete self-improvement cycle works
        2. test_value_score_increases - Value scores increase after improvement
        3. test_rollback_on_failure - Automatic rollback when validation fails
        4. test_protected_file_rejection - Protected files require higher consensus
        5. test_rate_limiting - Rate limiting prevents runaway evolution
        <Class TestFirstSelfImprovement>
          Test that the system can perform a complete self-improvement cycle.
          
          This validates the core recursive evolution loop:
          - Set up a mock swarm file with intentional inefficiency
          - Run full evolution cycle
          - Verify improvement was applied
          - Verify tests still pass after improvement
          <Function test_first_self_improvement_complete_cycle>
            Test complete self-improvement cycle from detection to application.
            
            Validates:
            1. System detects inefficiency in file
            2. System generates improvement proposal
            3. System validates the proposal
            4. System achieves consensus
            5. System applies the improvement
            6. Tests still pass after improvement
          <Function test_first_self_improvement_detects_issues>
            Test that the introspection phase correctly detects inefficiencies.
        <Class TestValueScoreIncreases>
          Test that value scores correctly track improvement metrics.
          
          Note: The composite score formula includes improvement potential (1-coverage),
          so increased coverage reduces improvement_score. We test that:
          - Component scores reflect actual improvements
          - Complexity and staleness reductions improve scores
          - The tracker correctly measures deltas over time
          <Function test_value_score_complexity_reduction_improves_score>
            Test that complexity reduction improves the complexity score.
          <Function test_value_score_staleness_reduction_improves_score>
            Test that staleness reduction (recent modification) improves score.
          <Function test_value_score_coverage_increases_improvement_potential_decreases>
            Test that higher coverage reduces improvement potential (as expected).
          <Function test_evolution_tracker_measures_improvement_delta>
            Test that EvolutionTracker correctly measures and accumulates improvement deltas.
          <Function test_value_score_tracked_over_multiple_cycles>
            Test that value scores are tracked correctly over multiple improvement cycles.
            Uses external value scores that increase to simulate improvement.
        <Class TestRollbackOnFailure>
          Test automatic rollback when validation fails.
          
          Validates:
          - System attempts improvement
          - Validation fails
          - Original code is automatically restored
          - No partial changes remain
          <Function test_rollback_on_validation_failure>
            Test that rollback occurs when validation fails.
          <Function test_rollback_restores_original_content>
            Test that rollback restores the original file content.
          <Function test_multiple_iterations_with_rollback>
            Test that system handles multiple failed iterations with rollback.
        <Class TestProtectedFileRejection>
          Test that protected files require higher consensus.
          
          Validates:
          - Marking a file as protected
          - Rejection when consensus < 90%
          - Approval when consensus >= 90%
          <Function test_protected_file_requires_higher_consensus>
            Test that protected files require 90% consensus (vs 75% for normal files).
          <Function test_protected_file_approved_with_high_consensus>
            Test that protected files are approved with >= 90% consensus.
          <Function test_normal_file_approved_with_lower_consensus>
            Test that normal (non-protected) files can be approved with 75% consensus.
          <Function test_axion_core_protected_file_selection>
            Test that AxionCore respects protected files in selection.
        <Class TestRateLimiting>
          Test that rate limiting prevents runaway evolution.
          
          Validates:
          - Rate limit is enforced
          - Cooldown period between improvements
          - Rate limit resets over time
          <Function test_rate_limit_enforced>
            Test that rate limit prevents exceeding max improvements per hour.
          <Function test_cooldown_period_applied>
            Test that cooldown period is applied between improvements.
          <Function test_rate_limit_tracks_by_hour>
            Test that rate limit properly tracks improvements per hour window.
          <Function test_evolution_tracker_velocity>
            Test that EvolutionTracker correctly calculates velocity for rate limiting.
        <Class TestFullSystemValidation>
          Comprehensive validation that all components work together correctly.
          <Function test_full_system_e2e_with_all_features>
            End-to-end test exercising all validation features together.

      <Module test_value_evaluator.py>
        Tests for ValueEvaluator - AI value scoring for recursive evolution.
        
        TDD Tests:
        1. Test ValueEvaluator initializes with default weights
        2. Test compute_access_score - high count -> high score (log scale)
        3. Test compute_criticality_score - many dependents -> high criticality
        4. Test compute_improvement_score - low coverage -> high improvement potential
        5. Test evaluate() returns ValueScore with composite_score in [0,1]
        <Class TestValueEvaluatorInit>
          Test ValueEvaluator initialization.
          <Function test_default_weights>

    <Module test_geoasm_encoder.py>
      Tests for GeoASM Semantic Encoder

      - Green for source register
      - Blue for destination register
      - Alpha for immediate value
      <Class TestGeoASMSemanticEncoder>

        <Function test_attend_has_highest_red_value>

        <Function test_layer_has_lowest_red_value>

          Verify heat intensity ordering from highest to lowest.
        <Function test_green_channel_source_register>

        <Function test_blue_channel_destination_register>

        <Function test_alpha_channel_immediate_value>
          Alpha channel should directly encode immediate value (0-255).

    <Module test_heatmap_e2e.py>
      End-to-end test for Heat Map visualization.
      
      Test Flow:
      1. Start Visual Bridge with Heat Aggregator
      2. Connect as a WebSocket client
      3. Record simulated access patterns
      4. Verify heat_map_update broadcast messages
      5. Validate the full pipeline from recording to frontend
      
      Architecture:
      +------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+
      | Test Client      |     | Visual Bridge    |     | Heat Aggregator  |
      | (WebSocket)      | <--> | (Port 8768)      | --> | (1 Hz updates)   |
      +------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+
                                      |
                                      v
                               Test Assertions
      <Coroutine test_heatmap_e2e_basic_broadcast>

        Steps:
        1. Connect to Visual Bridge via WebSocket
        2. Record access patterns via HeatAggregator
        3. Verify heat_map_update messages are received

    <Module test_model_extractor.py>
      <UnitTestCase TestModelExtractor>
        A class whose instances are single test cases.
        
        By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
        'runTest'.
        
        If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
        many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
        subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
        that the instance is to execute.
        
        Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
        and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
        implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
        
        If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
        __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
        should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
        of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
        in order to be run.
        
        When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
        * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
            the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
            exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
        * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
            objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
            to any explicit message passed.
        * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
            by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
            attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
        <TestCaseFunction test_different_model_types>
          Test extraction with different model types.
        <TestCaseFunction test_extract_weights_basic>
          Test basic weight extraction with mock GPT-Neo state dict.
        <TestCaseFunction test_weight_layout_sectors>
          Test that weight layout sectors are correctly defined.
        <TestCaseFunction test_weight_ordering>
          Test that weights are extracted in the correct order.

    <Module test_tectonic_district.py>

      <Class TestVectorSimilarityCosine>

          State should derive to HIGH_COHESION for cohesion >= 0.7.

          State should derive to DORMANT for 0.4 <= cohesion < 0.7.

      <Module test_resource_allocator.py>
        Unit tests for ResourceAllocator
        
        Tests resource allocation for multi-boot infrastructure including:
        - VNC port allocation from 5900-5999 range
        - Socket path generation
        - Collision prevention
        - Port reuse after release
        - Thread safety
        - Edge cases (exhaustion, invalid names)
        <Class TestBasicAllocation>
          Tests for basic allocation functionality.
          <Function test_allocate_returns_unique_ports>
            Each allocation returns a unique VNC port.
          <Function test_allocate_creates_socket_paths>
            Allocation creates serial and monitor socket paths.
          <Function test_allocate_returns_allocated_resources>
            Allocation returns AllocatedResources with all fields populated.
          <Function test_allocate_same_name_returns_same_resources>
            Allocating same name twice returns same resources (idempotent).
        <Class TestCollisionPrevention>
          Tests for preventing resource collisions.
          <Function test_multiple_allocations_no_collisions>
            Multiple allocations never have colliding ports.
          <Function test_deterministic_allocation>
            Same name always gets same container ID (deterministic).
        <Class TestReleaseAndReuse>
          Tests for resource release and reuse.
          <Function test_release_allows_reuse>
            Released ports can be reallocated.
          <Function test_release_removes_from_tracking>
            Released resources are removed from tracking.
          <Function test_socket_cleanup_on_release>
            Release clears socket tracking.
        <Class TestPortExhaustion>
          Tests for port exhaustion handling.
          <Function test_port_exhaustion_raises_error>

          <Function test_available_count_decreases>
            Available count decreases as ports are allocated.
        <Class TestThreadSafety>
          Tests for thread-safe allocation.
          <Function test_concurrent_allocation_thread_safety>
            Concurrent allocations don't cause collisions.
          <Function test_concurrent_release_thread_safety>
            Concurrent releases don't cause issues.
        <Class TestInvalidNames>
          Tests for name validation.
          <Function test_invalid_name_empty>
            Empty name raises InvalidNameError.
          <Function test_invalid_name_none>
            None name raises InvalidNameError.
          <Function test_invalid_name_special_chars>
            Names with invalid characters raise InvalidNameError.
          <Function test_valid_names_accepted>

          Tests for clear_all functionality.
          <Function test_clear_all_resets_state>
            clear_all resets allocator to initial state.

      <Module test_snapshot_committer.py>
        Unit tests for SnapshotCommitter class.
        
        Tests the commit pipeline orchestration with mocked dependencies:
        - BootBridge: Provides VM access and snapshot creation
        - QemuBoot: Provides QEMU monitor commands
        - VMSnapshotManager: Creates internal snapshots
        - QemuImgWrapper: Extracts snapshots to files
        
        Key test scenarios:
        - Full pipeline success
        - Verify order: create snapshot -> pause -> extract -> resume
        - VM always resumed after success
        - VM always resumed after failure
        - Auto-generated tags
        - Custom tags
        - Progress callbacks

      <UnitTestCase TestGeoCCAssembly>
        A class whose instances are single test cases.
        
        By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
        'runTest'.
        
        If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
        many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
        subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
        that the instance is to execute.
        
        Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
        and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
        implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
        
        If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
        __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
        should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
        of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
        in order to be run.
        
        When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
        * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
            the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
            exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
        * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
            objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
            to any explicit message passed.
        * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
            by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
            attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
        <TestCaseFunction test_invalid_opcode_throws_error>

        'runTest'.

        many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
        subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
        that the instance is to execute.
        
        Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
        and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
        implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
        
        If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
        __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
        should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
        of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
        in order to be run.
        
        When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
        * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
            the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
            exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
        * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
            objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
            to any explicit message passed.
        * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
            by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
            attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
        <TestCaseFunction test_rot_basic_cases>
          Test basic Hilbert rotation cases
        <TestCaseFunction test_rot_corner_cases>
          Test rotation at corners
        <TestCaseFunction test_rot_with_ry_one>
          Test rotation when ry=1
        <TestCaseFunction test_rot_with_ry_zero>
          Test rotation when ry=0

        many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
        subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
        that the instance is to execute.
        
        Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
        and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
        implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
        
        If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
        __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
        should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
        of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
        in order to be run.
        
        When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
        * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
            the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
            exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
        * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
            objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
            to any explicit message passed.
        * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
            by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
            attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.

        By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
        'runTest'.
        
        If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
        many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
        subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
        that the instance is to execute.
        
        Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
        and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
        implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.
        
        If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
        __init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
        should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
        of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
        in order to be run.
        
        When subclassing TestCase, you can set these attributes:
        * failureException: determines which exception will be raised when
            the instance's assertion methods fail; test methods raising this
            exception will be deemed to have 'failed' rather than 'errored'.
        * longMessage: determines whether long messages (including repr of
            objects used in assert methods) will be printed on failure in *addition*
            to any explicit message passed.
        * maxDiff: sets the maximum length of a diff in failure messages
            by assert methods using difflib. It is looked up as an instance
            attribute so can be configured by individual tests if required.
